businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and commercial AI data analytics platform leader — three-pillar system of Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir provides data integration, analytics, and AI decision-making platforms for government and commercial organizations. FY2025 revenue $4.48B (est. +70% YoY), adjusted operating margin 57%. Tuesday close $151.14 (-3.38%). Declined against broad market tech rally amid sustained insider selling (Karp/Thiel $400M+). Forbes profiles as potential 'new leader of the AI Revolution.' Market cap $361.5B, P/E 239.90x, Forward P/E 114.35x.
howTheyMakeMoney: Government contracts: Gotham/Apollo — defense, intelligence agencies, NATO (~55% of revenue)
Commercial contracts: Foundry/AIP — enterprise AI platform, Boot Camp strategy (~45% of revenue)
SaaS model: Annual subscription + expansion-based. Net Dollar Retention 120%+

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on EPS $0.63. Top-tier premium even within AI sector

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026E EPS $1.32. Still elevated but reflects growth

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 estimate. Accelerating on AI demand explosion

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: 57% on adjusted basis. SaaS leverage effect

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: High return on equity. Capital-efficient growth

growthDrivers.title: Revenue +70% YoY Acceleration — AI Boot Camp Strategy Bears Fruit
growthDrivers.description: AIP Boot Camp customer acquisition strategy delivering results. Companies transitioning from AI pilot to enterprise-wide deployment. Net Dollar Retention 120%+ maintained.

growthDrivers.title: US-Iran Tensions → Defense Demand Surge
growthDrivers.description: Heightened geopolitical tensions drive surging Department of Defense demand for Gotham platform. Palantir's battlefield AI and data analytics capabilities highlighted as critical assets.

growthDrivers.title: Forbes Profiles as Potential 'New Leader of AI Revolution'
growthDrivers.description: With +84% YoY stock gains, Forbes spotlights Palantir's AI leadership position. Real-world AI deployment experience identified as key differentiator.

growthDrivers.title: Adjusted Operating Margin 57% — SaaS Leverage Kicking In
growthDrivers.description: Cost growth contained relative to revenue growth, maximizing leverage effect. FCF margin 46.94% demonstrates excellent cash generation.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ year track record in government/defense AI. Security clearances create entry barriers. Expanding TAM via commercial AIP platform.

moats.description: Top-level US DoD and intelligence agency security clearances. Competitors cannot enter.
Real-world deployment experience including Ukraine conflict. MetaConstellation satellite analysis.
5-day intensive pilot to win enterprise customers, then expand organization-wide. High conversion rates.

competitors.name: Microsoft (Azure AI)
competitors.detail: Cloud AI platform. General-purpose approach vs Palantir's specialized analytics

competitors.name: Snowflake
competitors.detail: Data lakehouse. Some overlap in analytics domain

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI competitor. Inferior in scale and profitability

competitors.name: Anduril
competitors.detail: Defense AI startup. Growing rapidly but smaller than Palantir

recentNews.headline: PLTR -3.38% — drops against tech rally amid sustained insider selling
recentNews.significance: Despite Nasdaq rally, Palantir declined. CEO Karp and Director Thiel's $400M+ stock sales weigh on investor sentiment.

recentNews.headline: Forbes: 'Is Palantir the New Leader of the AI Revolution?'
recentNews.significance: With +84% one-year returns, Forbes examines Palantir's AI leadership position. Real-world AI deployment experience highlighted as differentiator.

recentNews.headline: Peter Thiel sells $400M+ in PLTR stock — 'He may regret it'
recentNews.significance: Seeking Alpha analysis: Thiel's 2M share sale represents only 2% of holdings. Revenue +70%, adjusted margin 57% show fundamentals intact.

recentNews.headline: PLTR surges +4.72% — US-Iran tensions boost defense demand expectations
recentNews.significance: Geopolitical tensions drive expectations for defense AI platform demand surge. Rose against same-day tech selloff.

recentNews.headline: DNB Asset Management sells 412,349 PLTR shares
recentNews.significance: Institutional investor exit signal, though negligible relative to total float.

analystOpinions.notableComment: 16 of 28 analysts rate Buy. 24/7 Wall St target $161.91 (conservative), Wedbush Dan Ives calls 'garage sale prices' (bullish). Growth acknowledged but valuation debate ongoing.

risks.title: Sustained Large-Scale Insider Selling
risks.description: CEO Karp and Director Thiel sold $400M+ between 2/20-3/3. Possible signal that insiders view stock as overvalued.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation — P/E 239.90x
risks.description: Forward P/E 114.35x is highest in the AI sector. PEG 2.65. Multiple contraction risk if growth decelerates.

risks.title: Government Budget Dependency
risks.description: ~55% of revenue from government contracts. Vulnerable to budget cuts, administration changes, and policy shifts.

risks.title: Cloud Giant AI Platform Competition
risks.description: Microsoft Azure AI, AWS SageMaker entering enterprise AI market. Long-term commercial revenue erosion possible.

risks.title: Share Dilution
risks.description: SBC-driven +4.67% YoY share count increase. Long-term EPS dilution effect.

buyReasons.title: Revenue +70% YoY Acceleration — AI Platform Leader Position
buyReasons.rationale: AIP Boot Camp strategy driving rapid commercial customer growth. Dual government + commercial growth expanding TAM.

buyReasons.title: FCF Margin 46.94% + Net Cash $6.95B
buyReasons.rationale: Excellent cash generation. Near debt-free balance sheet provides top-tier financial stability.

buyReasons.title: Structural Defense AI Demand Growth
buyReasons.rationale: US-Iran tensions, Ukraine conflict — geopolitical instability structurally expanding defense AI investment.

buyReasons.title: Trading Below 50-Day SMA ($155.81) — Testing Bounce Point
buyReasons.rationale: RSI 53.98 neutral. Testing support near 50-day SMA. Re-entry opportunity on bounce.

overallOpinion: Palantir closed at $151.14 (-3.38%), declining against the broad tech rally on Tuesday. CEO Karp and Director Thiel's $400M+ insider sales are directly pressuring the stock.
Fundamentals remain strong. Revenue +70% YoY acceleration, adjusted operating margin 57%, FCF margin 47%. Forbes profiles it as the potential 'new leader of the AI Revolution.'

The problem is valuation. P/E of 239.90x and Forward P/E of 114.35x are burdensome even accounting for +70% growth. The message from insider selling and PEG 2.65 is clear — 'great company, expensive stock.' Trading below both 50-day and 200-day SMAs, with $150 support being the key near-term directional indicator.

sources.name: Seeking Alpha
sources.description: Peter Thiel sale analysis — Strong Buy maintained

threads.text: Karp + Thiel sell $400M+ while stock drops to $151. Yet revenue is +70%, margins at 57%, FCF margin 47%. Forbes calls it an 'AI leader.' P/E of 240x is the only problem — the company itself is fine. What would you do? 👉 investory.kro.kr/stock/PLTR/analysis/2026-03-11

telegram.hook: 🔻 PLTR $151.14 (-3.38%) — Insider Selling vs AI Leader Narrative
telegram.text:
Palantir bucked the tech rally, dropping alone. CEO Karp and Director Thiel sold $400M+ in stock between 2/20-3/3.

But the fundamentals? Revenue +70% YoY, adjusted operating margin 57%, FCF margin 47%. Forbes is profiling it as the 'new leader of the AI Revolution.'

The real issue is P/E at 240x. Forward P/E of 114x is the highest in the AI sector. Great company, expensive stock — even insiders seem to agree.

Trading below both 50-day and 200-day SMAs. $150 support is the key.

Full analysis 👉 investory.kro.kr/stock/PLTR/analysis/2026-03-11
